idea: Utility Grid Strain Heat Wave Demand Spike

data.source: Open-Meteo daily temperature anomalies and FRED electricity load series (API EIA_ELECTRICITY_DEMAND)

behavior: When regional max temperatures exceed 95°F for 3+ consecutive days AND EIA electricity demand rises above its 30-day rolling average by >2%, grid strain intensifies and utilities face margin compression.

market_impact.rationale: Utilities see margin compression during demand spikes without proportional rate relief; operating costs rise faster than revenue.

trade_logic.entry: Max temp >95°F for 3 consecutive days AND EIA demand >102% of 30-day rolling mean
trade_logic.exit: Temperature falls below 90°F or EIA demand normalizes below 100.5% of rolling mean, or after 10 trading days

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Summer heat waves occur reliably; electricity demand data updates daily. Signal fires multiple times annually during June–September.

idea: Freight Volume Collapse On Weak Trucking Orders

data.source: FRED series TOTALSA (total truck tonnage and carloads) via API

behavior: When weekly truck tonnage declines >3% week-over-week for 2 consecutive weeks, logistics providers face utilization collapse and pricing pressure. Industrial demand weakens ahead of broader slowdown.

market_impact.rationale: Trucking tonnage is a lead indicator of industrial production and capex demand; sustained declines signal margin compression across industrials and transportation.

trade_logic.entry: Weekly tonnage MoM decline >3% for 2 consecutive weeks; cumulative 2-week decline >6%
trade_logic.exit: Tonnage rebounds >1% week-over-week or 15 trading days elapsed

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Freight tonnage is volatile; 3% weekly swings occur 4–6 times annually. Signal fires regularly outside holiday periods.

idea: EV Charger Installation Slowdown Sentiment Fade

data.source: OpenChargeMap API monthly charger count growth; cross-reference with FRED vehicle sales series

behavior: When weekly EV charger installation growth (count delta) falls >15% below its 12-week rolling average, it signals EV infrastructure investment cooling. Sentiment on clean energy capex fades.

market_impact.rationale: EV charger slowdown signals weakening clean tech capex and consumer EV adoption confidence, pressuring semiconductor and software exposure in energy transition.

trade_logic.entry: Weekly charger net adds fall >15% below 12-week rolling average; close below prior week close
trade_logic.exit: Weekly adds rebound above 10-week rolling average or 12 trading days elapsed

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: EV charging growth is lumpy; seasonality and policy cycles cause >15% swings 3–4 times per year. Signal matures in 2–3 weeks.

idea: Recession Whisper Google Trends Unemployment Search Surge

data.source: Google Trends weekly search volume for terms: 'unemployment benefits', 'job loss', 'layoffs near me'

behavior: When combined search interest in unemployment + layoff terms spikes >40% above 52-week baseline in a single week, consumer anxiety peaks. Historical lead to risk-off rotation.

market_impact.rationale: Unemployment search spikes precede consumer spending weakness; discretionary retailers face demand headwinds 2–4 weeks later.

trade_logic.entry: Combined normalized search score >140 (baseline 100) and >40% above 52-week mean
trade_logic.exit: Score falls below 120 or 20 trading days elapsed

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Unemployment search interest spikes 4–5 times annually during earnings seasons and macro shock periods; low barrier to fire.

idea: Airline Load Factor Stress Pricing Power Collapse

data.source: BTS airline load factor and available seat miles via FRED/BTS adapter, weekly series

behavior: When weekly airline load factor (% seats filled) falls >2.5% from prior 4-week rolling average while available seat miles remain flat, pricing power collapses. Carriers cut fares and margins compress.

market_impact.rationale: Load factor collapse signals consumer travel demand softness and forces airlines to discount capacity, eroding margins and reducing equity valuations.

trade_logic.entry: Weekly load factor >2.5% below 4-week rolling average AND seat miles YoY flat to +1%
trade_logic.exit: Load factor rebounds above 4-week rolling mean or 14 trading days elapsed

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Airline load factors fluctuate seasonally; 2.5% swings occur 6–8 times annually. Trigger is sensitive enough to fire multiple times per quarter.

idea: Wage Growth Demand Surge Fast Food Google Trends

data.source: Google Trends weekly search volume for 'fast food hiring', 'minimum wage increase', 'restaurant worker pay'

behavior: When combined search interest spikes >35% above 52-week baseline, it signals labor tightness in low-wage sectors. Restaurant and hospitality operators face cost inflation; profit margins compress.

market_impact.rationale: Fast food wage pressure forces menu price increases; consumer traffic declines or transaction sizes stall, compressing restaurant operator margins.

trade_logic.entry: Combined normalized search score >135 (baseline 100) sustained for 2 consecutive weeks
trade_logic.exit: Score falls below 115 or 18 trading days elapsed

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Fast food hiring/wage searches spike seasonally (spring/summer) and during policy announcements; fires 5–7 times per year.

idea: Commodity Input Cost Shock Mining Sector Margin Crunch

data.source: FRED commodity price indices (energy, metals, agricultural) daily series; XLB ETF daily price via price_only adapter

behavior: When FRED commodity index (broad basket) spikes >3% in a single day while XLB stays flat or declines, input costs surge faster than mining/materials producer pricing. Margin squeeze imminent.

market_impact.rationale: Materials producers cannot immediately pass through input cost shocks; operating margins compress until prices normalize or they cut production.

trade_logic.entry: Commodity index +3% day AND XLB +0.5% or lower same day
trade_logic.exit: XLB closes >1.5% above entry close or 8 trading days elapsed

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Daily commodity shocks >3% occur 2–3 times per month. Divergence with materials ETF pricing happens 4–6 times annually.

idea: Real Estate Cap Rate Compression Yield Chase Unwind

data.source: FRED commercial real estate cap rate series (MORTGAGE30US, TERMCBCCALLNS) daily; XLRE ETF daily price via price_only adapter

behavior: When 10-year mortgage rates fall >15 bps in a week but XLRE underperforms SPY by >1.5%, yield-chasing capital rotates away from real estate. Sentiment on cap rate compression fades.

market_impact.rationale: Rate cuts typically boost REIT valuations; when they don't, it signals investor skepticism about fundamental demand or dividend sustainability.

trade_logic.entry: 10-year mortgage rate falls >15 bps weekly AND XLRE underperforms SPY by >1.5% over same period
trade_logic.exit: XLRE outperforms SPY >1% or 12 trading days elapsed

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Mortgage rates are volatile; 15 bps weekly moves occur 8–10 times annually. Signal matures quickly and fires 3–4 times per quarter.

idea: Bank Deposit Outflow Fear Inversion Signal

data.source: FRED series on bank deposits (BMNRNJ) and high-yield savings rate (MMNRNJ) weekly; XLF ETF daily price via price_only adapter

behavior: When weekly bank deposit flows turn negative (outflows exceed inflows) for 2 consecutive weeks AND high-yield savings rates spike >0.5% above prior month, retail depositors flee traditional banks. Bank funding stress accelerates.

market_impact.rationale: Deposit outflows force banks to raise rates, compress NIM, and rely on wholesale funding. Equity valuations contract on net interest margin compression.

trade_logic.entry: Weekly deposit growth <0% for 2 consecutive weeks AND HY savings rate spike >0.5% MoM
trade_logic.exit: Deposit growth returns >0.5% or 15 trading days elapsed

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Deposit volatility and HY rate spreads move 4–6 times per year; signal fires reliably during rate hike cycles.

idea: Weather-Driven Agricultural Futures Shock Consumer Food Inflation

data.source: Open-Meteo precipitation and temperature daily data for US grain belt; cross-reference FRED agricultural commodity prices weekly

behavior: When grain belt precipitation falls >50% below normal for 14+ consecutive days AND FRED agricultural commodity indices spike >2.5% in a single week, food input costs surge. Consumer staples pricing power tested.

market_impact.rationale: Food inflation pressures staples consumers; if staples raise prices too much, volume declines. If they absorb costs, margins compress. Either way, equity returns suffer.

trade_logic.entry: Grain belt precip <50% of 30-year normal for 14+ days AND ag commodity index +2.5% weekly
trade_logic.exit: Precip rebounds to >70% normal or commodity index declines >1% or 20 trading days elapsed

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal dry spells and commodity spikes occur predictably; signal fires 3–5 times per year, especially during growing season (May–August).
